goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

full_morning_briefing.definition: Evaluate the morning_message together with the same-day generated_actions, because both are shown to the user as one morning briefing experience.
full_morning_briefing.morning_message:
Good morning, Ameena.

You said you're feeling pretty solid at 77/100 with high energy mode this morning — that's great to hear. Last night you got **8.95 hours** of sleep, which is actually longer than your usual, and your sleep efficiency was strong at 89%. The interesting thing is that while you slept plenty, your REM and deep sleep percentages were both low at just 10.8% each — your body was in bed but maybe not getting the deepest restorative stages it could.

Here's where it gets really telling: yesterday your subjective energy crashed to **18/100** while your physiological recovery (HRV at 119.5) was actually excellent. That's a huge 50-point gap between how you feel and what your body's showing — and it's been happening for three days straight. You also had zero moderate activity yesterday and high sedentary time (888 minutes), which creates this weird mismatch where your body has recovery capacity but you feel completely drained. The 'Tired but Content' emotion you reported captures that perfectly — pleasant exhaustion.

What's working? Your nutrition was stable with good protein (89.5g) and sensible caffeine timing. Your HRV being that high means your nervous system has solid recovery potential waiting to be tapped.

Today is about bridging that gap between your body's readiness and your energy perception. The actions focus on gentle movement to counter the sedentary spiral and reinforcing sleep structure to help those restorative stages.

You've got the physiological foundation — today's about activating it.

generated_actions.title: Set a 40-minute 'stand and move' timer during your first deep work block
fallbacks: If standing isn't possible, do seated ankle circles and neck rolls for 30 seconds
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's activity was critically poor with 0 MVPA, low steps (3810), and high sedentary minutes (888). Your 'tight schedule' and 'high workload' make prolonged sitting likely. This timed, tiny movement directly counters the sedentary spiral and activity-recovery decoupling identified in the holistic report, and varies from recent 'walk after lunch' suggestions by being anchored to a work timer in the morning.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 40-minute timer to take a standing movement break during your first deep work block?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports setting the timer and taking at least one standing micro-break when it chimed, disrupting prolonged sitting.

assumptions: You have a timer/phone
You can pause work briefly
You can stand and move safely
generated_actions.description: When you begin your most focused work session this morning, set a timer for 40 minutes. When it chimes, stand up and walk to a window or different room, do 30 seconds of light marching in place and shoulder rolls, then return to work.

must_avoid: staying seated for over 45 minutes continuously

generated_actions.title: At lunch, add a palm-sized protein source referencing yesterday's chicken milanese
fallbacks: If chicken unavailable, add a boiled egg, a large spoon of Greek yogurt, or a handful of nuts
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's nutrition was adequate in calories and protein (89.5g), but dinner was missing and confidence is moderate (0.61). This meal-aware action references your actual lunch from yesterday (chicken milanese) to promote consistency, directly addresses the 'ensure adequate protein intake' soft constraint, and supports your high energy mode and recovery needs.

signal_refs.label: protein at least as high as yesterday's 89.5g

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you intentionally add a substantial protein source to your lunch today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports enhancing lunch with protein, and today's total protein intake meets or exceeds yesterday's good level of 89.5g to support high energy mode and recovery.

assumptions: You will have a lunch meal
You have access to chicken or another protein source
generated_actions.description: When you have lunch today, intentionally include a portion of chicken (like yesterday's chicken milanese) or another lean protein (e.g., grilled chicken, fish, tofu) alongside your meal, aiming for at least 25g of protein in that sitting.

must_avoid: having only carb-heavy items like pasta or sweets

generated_actions.title: Set a 9:30 PM 'digital sunset' alarm and listen to calming audio for 10 minutes
fallbacks: If 10 minutes feels long, do 5 minutes but maintain the screen boundary
If no audio available, read a physical book or magazine in dim light
generated_actions.rationale: Last night's sleep onset (22:32) remains volatile with a sharply positive slope (+47.75). Sleep was long and efficient but light on restorative stages. This screen boundary directly addresses the 'volatile sleep onset trend' and 'prioritize wind-down routine' soft constraints, serves your goal domain of sleep by creating structure to prevent repeat of schedule instability, and varies from recent 'read a book' suggestions by using audio to reduce visual stimulation.

signal_refs.label: sleep onset earlier than last night's 22:32

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you honor the 9:30 PM screen boundary and listen to calming audio for 10 minutes?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports avoiding screens after 9:30 PM and engaging in screen-free audio relaxation, aiming for earlier sleep onset than last night's 22:32 to address schedule volatility.

assumptions: You have a smartphone with alarms
You have access to headphones or a speaker
You have dim lighting available
generated_actions.description: When your phone alarm goes off at 9:30 PM tonight, immediately put away all screens (phone, laptop, TV). Spend the next 10 minutes listening to calming instrumental music, a podcast, or an audiobook in dim light, without looking at any screens.

generated_actions.title: After your evening meal, have a caffeine-free herbal tea and set a 10:15 PM 'lights dim' reminder
fallbacks: If no herbal tea, drink warm water with lemon
If reminder is missed, dim lights as soon as you remember
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday you successfully avoided caffeine after 2pm (caffeine_after_2pm false). Building on that positive pattern, this action reinforces the 'reinforce early caffeine cutoff' soft constraint with a calming evening ritual and adds a light-dim reminder to address the 'volatile sleep onset trend'. It directly serves your sleep goal by creating multi-layered environmental cues for earlier, more stable sleep onset.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you have a caffeine-free herbal tea after your evening meal and dim lights at 10:15 PM?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports consuming a caffeine-free warm drink post-meal and intentionally reducing ambient light at 10:15 PM to support circadian rhythm and sleep onset.

assumptions: You have access to herbal tea or a caffeine-free warm beverage
You have a smartphone with reminders
You can control lighting
generated_actions.description: When you finish your evening meal, prepare a cup of caffeine-free herbal tea (e.g., chamomile, mint). Set a phone reminder for 10:15 PM to dim all lights in your living space and begin winding down in low light.

morning_briefing.morning_message:

You said you're feeling pretty solid at 77/100 with high energy mode this morning — that's great to hear. assumptions: User can safely perform light exercise and morning basketball practice
User will have access to meals and protein sources
User has a timer/alarm for implementing structured breaks
No medical contraindications for suggested activities
soft_constraints: monitor energy score (18) vs. overall high score mismatch
address subjective-objective mismatch (gap 50) with low confidence in journal data
reinforce early caffeine cutoff (no caffeine after 2pm observed yesterday)
prioritize wind-down routine before late bedtime given volatile sleep onset trend
ensure adequate protein intake to support high energy mode and recovery

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena! 

You're starting today feeling strong with an 86/100 — that's your own high-energy read on the morning. First, the basics: **you slept 6.5 hours last night with excellent efficiency (90.8%)**, meaning your rest was solid and consolidated. That's a great foundation. Your body's recovery signals are actually outstanding — your HRV hit 121ms, which is a sharp jump up and shows your nervous system is primed for calm. 

Here's the interesting tension: yesterday you felt *critically* low energy (19/100) and tired, despite that strong physiological backdrop and yesterday's sleep-quality rating being fine. Your activity was low (0 MVPA, 3,270 steps) and nutrition dipped sharply — only 1,060 calories across two meals. When low movement, low fuel, and high sedentary time (730 minutes) compound, it creates a spiral where your mind and body feel disconnected, even if your HRV says you're recovered. That's the big story: your engine is tuned, but the tank was nearly empty yesterday. 

Today is about reconnecting those signals. The theme is **small, intentional resets** — using your built-in physiological strength to support your energy and focus where yesterday lagged. 

You've got the recovery capital; today's about spending it wisely on movement, nourishment, and breath.

evidence_quotes: I am still recovering from my cold and half of my head feels heavy

meal_descriptions.description: One brown toast with labneh and one brown toast with red cheddar and an instant coffee
A spoon of beetroot salad, a slice of pizza with sausage, half a chicken milanese and half a plate of pasta eith red sauce and sausage
